_doc: Backend operations available to the Population Atlas. There is NO population-private server (no atlases/population/server/...); the atlas calls the shared atlas-core server at atlas-core/server/atlas_server.py PLUS the atlas-specific sidecar router atlas-core/server/population_endpoint.py (mounted under /api/population/, mirrors atlas-core/server/diversity_endpoint.py). Round 2 wiring (2026-05-14) registers (a) the four atlas-core compute endpoints the Population Atlas can use right now (popstats_groupwise, popstats_hobs_groupwise, ancestry_groupwise_q, actions_submit) plus generic file/layer plumbing; (b) the population_endpoint.py JSON-shim slots that surface each file-backed layer at a stable /api/population/<slot> URL. ENDPOINT URLS AND REQUEST SHAPES ARE AUDITED AGAINST the actual Python source — do NOT invent endpoints; if a layer needs server compute, the endpoint must already exist there.
_round1_status_legacy: empty — no server-side compute (no longer accurate; see _round2_status).
_round2_status: Two-source server wiring: (1) shared atlas-core compute endpoints (popstats / ancestry / actions / layers) for groupwise summaries on page3; (2) the population_endpoint.py JSON-shim sidecar — read-only GETs that surface the cohort-level static JSON files at /api/population/<slot>. The sidecar mirrors atlases/diversity/'s pattern verbatim: a tiny APIRouter mounted from atlas_server.py via two lines (`from population_endpoint import make_population_router; app.include_router(make_population_router(_safe_project_path))`). The mount line is NOT YET in atlas_server.py at audit time (held back per session constraint — another chat is editing atlas_server.py). Until mounted, /api/population/<slot> returns 404 from the FastAPI router itself; the same files remain reachable via the generic GET /file/<path> fallback.

_server_audit_origin.audited_against: atlas-core HEAD on main (post popstats_server.py → atlas_server.py move; pre-population_endpoint.py mount)
_server_audit_origin._note: Endpoint line numbers below are 1-indexed at audit time. If a file is renumbered, the @app.<method>('<path>') anchor is still the source of truth — not the line number.

_server_connection_doc: The shared atlas-core server runs on LANTA. Reach it via SSH tunnel: 'ssh -L 8765:127.0.0.1:8765 lanta'. See atlas-core/server/SERVER_README.md.
_groupwise_contract: All three groupwise operations (popstats_groupwise, popstats_hobs_groupwise, ancestry_groupwise_q) enforce min_group_n (default 10) AGAINST THE FILTERED-TO-KNOWN sample list — unknown sample_ids are silently dropped, then the floor is applied. Group names must match [A-Za-z0-9_]+. Population-side callers SHOULD pass the NGSadmix K-clusters or NAToRA-pruned family clusters as the groups dict; whole-cohort calls pass a single group named 'all_226'.

popstats_groupwise._inputs_doc: Population Atlas typically groups by NGSadmix K-cluster assignment (page3) or by NAToRA-pruned family (page3 deep-dive). No activeChrom slot is wired in round 2 — callers pass the chrom string explicitly. The Inversion Atlas's activeChrom hand-off lands in round 3+.
popstats_groupwise._doc: FST + dXY + theta_pi for arbitrary group compositions. Page3 uses this to compute between-K-cluster FST when NGSadmix assignments are stable, and between-family FST when the family clusters from ngsRelate/NAToRA are stable. Up to 10 groups per request. Members validated against canonical sample list; unknowns dropped silently. Group names match [A-Za-z0-9_]+.

popstats_hobs_groupwise._inputs_doc: Same grouping convention as popstats_groupwise. Population Atlas calls this for cohort-summary H on page4 and for per-K-cluster H on page3.
popstats_hobs_groupwise._doc: Per-group HoverE (heterozygosity) via Mérot-patched ANGSD. First request for a novel group composition triggers an ANGSD run (~5–15 s per group). Subsequent same-composition requests <100 ms server-side. Page4 currently stays in cross-reference mode (depth lives in the Diversity Atlas); when page4 is upgraded to a live cohort-summary tile, it consumes this op.

ancestry_groupwise_q._inputs_doc: Page3 uses this to render per-family or per-K-cluster mean Q vectors as bar plots. K=8 is the canonical value per session memory; the Population Atlas also requests K=2..K_max for the NGSadmix sweep panel.
ancestry_groupwise_q._doc: Reads precomputed instant_q Q-matrix and aggregates per group. Pure file I/O + pandas group-by; ~50 ms uncached, <10 ms cached. Used by page3 NGSadmix-Q bar-plot panel to show how Q varies across families or across K-clusters.

request_shape.atlas_id: string, optional — defaults to ?atlas=population query param
request_shape.submitted_at: ISO8601 string, optional — server fills if omitted

actions_submit._inputs_doc: No slot inputs — the action manifest carries everything. Atlas resolution precedence: ?atlas=population query > manifest.atlas_id > master_config.atlas.active_atlas.
actions_submit._doc: Submit an action manifest. The atlas-core server validates, dispatches via the per-atlas dispatcher (atlases/population/registries/dispatcher.py — EXISTS as of this commit; one action registered: compute_ancestry_q), writes returned envelopes under data/_layers/, and appends a success/error entry to actions.log.jsonl. Returns {ok, action_id, atlas_id, produced_layers}. Population uses this to trigger ancestry-Q computation today, and (when more actions land) MODULE_QC re-runs and MODULE_2B re-runs (page3 'rerun NGSadmix at K=X' button).

actions_status._doc: Returns the latest log entry for action_id (the actions log is append-only; later writes win on read). 404 if no entry exists. Used by the Population Atlas to poll long-running re-runs submitted via actions_submit.

request_shape.stage: string, optional (one of: qc | structure | health | help)

layers_list._doc: Lists registered layer envelopes (most-recent last). Useful for showing 'available data layers' panels on page1 (samples) and page2 (QC). Filter by stage='structure' to see what page3 has access to.

layers_get._doc: Read the envelope JSON for a layer_id. Used by the layer-fetching client (_atlas_data_loader) when a layers.registry entry has source: 'envelope' rather than source: 'file'. All Population Atlas pages with declared upstream layers can use this; pages 8/9 use it to load hatchery_health_cohorts and per_sample_stats envelopes once those are produced by actions_submit.

response_shape.slots: list of string — sorted slot names
response_shape.templated_slots: list of string — slot names that take URL path params (currently only ['ngsadmix_q'])

population_slot_list._doc: Discovery endpoint: returns the canonical slot inventory. Used by the data loader to know which slots can be requested at /api/population/<slot>. Stable; safe to cache aggressively.

population_slot_get._wraps: static JSON file under PROJECT_ROOT (one of eight paths in SLOTS)

request_shape.slot: string — path param; one of per_sample_stats | family_clusters | inversion_carriers | marker_controls | hatchery_health_cohorts | pcangsd_pca | ngsrelate_kinship | module_qc_summary

population_slot_get.output_schema: see layers.registry.json — each slot's payload schema is the layer it feeds
population_slot_get._doc: JSON-shim GET — surfaces a static layer file under a stable URL keyed by slot name (rather than file path). 404 when the file is absent (caller falls back to scaffold 'data pending' empty state). 503 when the /file subsystem is down (no PROJECT_ROOT configured). Same contract as /api/diversity/<slot>; the slot inventory is enumerated in population_endpoint.py SLOTS and tested in test_population_endpoint.py.

population_ngsadmix_q._wraps: static JSON file data/ngsadmix/Q_K<k>.json under PROJECT_ROOT
population_ngsadmix_q._doc: JSON-shim GET for the K-templated NGSadmix slot. Separate route because K is part of the URL path. K must be in [2, 12] (NGSadmix sweep range; out-of-range values return 400 rather than 404 so caller mistakes surface). 404 when the Q_K<k>.json file is absent.

_planned_round3._doc: Population-Atlas-specific composites that do NOT yet have endpoints in atlas_server.py. These are contract entries: documented now so page-side code can stub against them, but the @app.post / @app.get bindings land in a later round.
_planned_round3._dispatcher_status_doc: atlases/population/registries/dispatcher.py EXISTS as of audit time. It loads actions.registry.json + extractors.registry.json from registries/data/ and routes manifests through runners (registries/runners/) and extractors (registries/extractors/). One action is registered today: compute_ancestry_q → runners.ancestry.compute_q → extractors.ancestry_q.extract → ancestry_q layer (schema staging_ancestry_q_v0). Additional planned actions are listed below.

compute_ancestry_q_action._doc: First registered Population-Atlas action. Submit a manifest with action_type='compute_ancestry_q' + params {chrom, scale, K, groups}; the dispatcher calls the runner which posts to /api/ancestry/groupwise_q, then the extractor parses the response into an ancestry_q layer envelope. Used by page3's NGSadmix Q-bar panel.

kinship_aggregate._doc: Wrap NAToRA pruning + family-tree clustering as a server compute. Today this runs in the catfish-population-analysis MODULE_2B pipeline (offline). Promotion would let the Population Atlas re-prune live at a user-specified threshold and see the family clusters update interactively.

cohort_summary_stats._doc: Produce the cohort-level headline numbers shown on the heterozygosity / diversity / inbreeding cross-reference cards (pages 4/5/6). Today these are hardcoded in the scaffold HTML; promoting to a server endpoint would let them update when MODULE_3 emits fresh per-sample tables.

hatchery_health_classify._doc: Classify the focal cohort into a quadrant on the F_ROH × H plane. Today the classification logic lives in page9.js (verbatim-lifted from the scaffold's _fhh* helpers). Promoting to a server endpoint would centralise the verdict so page8's auto-derivation hook and page9's plane share the same source of truth (today there is a hand-off via window.state._hatcheryHealth).

thresholds._doc: Override the default top-1% F_ROH / θπ / burden cutoffs.

breeding_highlights_derive._doc: Centralise page8's auto-derivation hooks (top-1% F_ROH/θπ/burden, largest family cluster, rare-haplotype carriers, marker positives/negatives) on the server. Today this runs in browser via the verbatim-lifted _bpDerive* helpers reading window.state.*. Promotion makes the breeding-highlights view deterministic and re-runnable from the action log.

_debug_endpoints_doc: The shared atlas-core server also exposes /api/cache/keys, /api/cache/keys/<hash> (DELETE), /api/jobs/<job_id>, /health, /api/health. These are not registered as Population-Atlas registry operations because pages don't consume them — they're for the developer console and the toolbar status badge (which polls /health every 30 s).

_engines_referenced._doc: The Population Atlas does NOT ship its own engines. All compute is delegated to the shared atlas-core server, which in turn calls C/C++ engines living outside the atlas (in unified_ancestry/, angsd_fixed_HWE/). The atlas-core operations registry is the canonical source for engine paths; this entry is informational only.
